## CHAR-9 — Sinful speech (189 rows: E=178 S=11 G=0 CS=0)

### T0

#### T0.1.1 (cf=18115, E) — What does the verse evidence reveal about the nature or character of God that this characteristic reflects or images?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Sinful speech reveals God's character through three lenses. (1) *God as the exclusive holder of divine prerogatives*: Mar 2:7 — "Who can forgive sins but God alone?" and Joh 10:33 — "you, being a man, make yourself God" — blasphemy is defined as the improper claiming of what belongs exclusively to God. The characteristic reveals God as the one whose authority, identity, and prerogatives are absolute and non-transferable. (2) *God as the one whose Spirit cannot be blasphemed without eternal consequence*: Mat 12:31 — "the blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven" — God's Spirit is the one reality against which blasphemous speech carries irreversible moral weight. (3) *God as the one against whom the beast's constitutive speech is directed*: Rev 13:6 — "It opened its mouth to utter blasphemies against God, blaspheming his name and his dwelling, that is, those who dwell in heaven" — God's name, presence, and people are the targets of the most extreme form of sinful speech.

---

#### T0.1.2 (cf=18116, S) — Does Scripture explicitly attribute this characteristic to God — and if so, what does that attribution reveal about its significance in the human person?

**[CHAR-9]** S — No verse in the 22-verse corpus attributes blasphemy or sinful speech to God. God appears as the target of blasphemy (Rev 13:6), the one whose authority blasphemy presumes to claim (Mar 2:7; Joh 10:33), and the one whose Spirit suffers the unforgivable form (Mat 12:31) — never as a blasphemer. The characteristic is exclusively creaturely in both its slander and its blasphemy registers.

---

#### T0.1.3 (cf=18117, E) — Where Scripture is silent about God's possession of this characteristic, what does that silence suggest about the characteristic's place in the divine image?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The silence on God blaspheming reveals two things. (1) God possesses speech — he speaks, names, declares, and pronounces — but his speech is always aligned with his own nature and truth; the creature's sinful speech is the perversion of the speech-capacity God gave. (2) Even the highest creaturely agents — angels — restrain blasphemous speech: Jude 9 — "even the archangel Michael... did not presume to pronounce a blasphemous judgment" and 2Pe 2:11 — "angels, though greater in might and power, do not pronounce a blasphemous judgment." If angelic speech is constrained from blasphemy, God's speech is infinitely more so. The silence confirms: sinful speech is the perversion of the image's speech-capacity, a capacity God gave and cannot himself misuse.

---

#### T0.2.1 (cf=18118, S) — What does the verse evidence suggest about the purpose this characteristic serves in the human person as created — what does it equip the person to be, do, or become?

**[CHAR-9]** S — The corpus provides no evidence of any positive created purpose for sinful speech. The 22 verses uniformly present it as originating in inner moral corruption (Mat 15:19), requiring renunciation (Eph 4:31; Col 3:8), flowing from diseased pride (1Ti 6:4), and constituting the beast's defiant identity (Rev 13:1/5/6). No verse presents sinful speech as serving any designed function.

---

#### T0.2.2 (cf=18119, E) — Does the evidence indicate whether this characteristic is part of the original created design, a response to the fallen condition, or both?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The evidence points to sinful speech as entirely belonging to the fallen condition. Mat 15:19 — "out of the heart come evil thoughts... slander" — the heart's corruption is the origin; the corruption is the fallen condition, not the original design. 1Ti 1:13 — Paul describes his former blasphemy as rooted in "ignorant unbelief" — unbelief is a fallen-condition state. 2Ti 3:2's list of end-times character traits (including abusive/blasphemous disposition) is explicitly presented as a mark of moral deterioration, not original design.

---

#### T0.2.3 (cf=18120, E) — Is there evidence that this characteristic is oriented toward a future fullness — something the person is moving toward, not only what they currently are?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The corpus evidences two eschatological trajectories. (1) *Judgment for unforgivable blasphemy*: Mat 12:31 — "the blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven" — the unforgivable form carries forward permanently into eschatological consequence. (2) *The beast's blasphemy as eschatological-concentrated evil*: Rev 13:5/6 — the beast utters blasphemous words for "forty-two months" — a defined eschatological period. The beast's constitutive blasphemy is oriented toward the judgment that the Revelation context anticipates. The trajectory of sinful speech is toward eschatological consequence rather than any positive fullness.

---

#### T0.3.1 (cf=18121, E) — In what way does this characteristic express the divine image in the human person — what aspect of being made in God's likeness does it instantiate?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Sinful speech perverts one of the image's most central capacities: the gift of speech. The image-bearer is a speaking creature — created to name (Gen 2:19–20, contextually), to declare, to praise, and to communicate moral truth. Mat 15:19 — "out of the heart come... slander" — the same heart-to-mouth channel designed for God-honouring speech is the channel through which slander flows. The image's speech-capacity is constitutively good; sinful speech is its inversion. Jude 9 and 2Pe 2:11 confirm that even the speech of angelic creatures is constrained from blasphemy — establishing that sinful speech is not merely a human failure but a creaturely violation of what speech is for.

---

#### T0.3.2 (cf=18122, S) — Does the evidence suggest that this characteristic is shared between God and the human person, or is it exclusively a creaturely analogue to something in God?

**[CHAR-9]** S — Exclusively creaturely (T0.1.2). God cannot blaspheme because blasphemy requires either false claims about God (which God cannot make about himself) or speech originating in inner moral corruption (which God does not possess).

---

#### T0.3.3 (cf=18123, E) — What does the presence or absence of this characteristic in a person reveal about the condition of the divine image in that person?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The presence of sinful speech reveals the image's condition across three levels. (1) *VCG-01 (slander from inner corruption)*: Mat 15:19 — "out of the heart come evil thoughts... slander" — sinful speech from inner corruption reveals the heart's own moral condition; the speech is a diagnostic of the heart's state. 1Ti 6:4 — slander arising from "conceit and unhealthy craving" — the image's inner moral centre is diseased. (2) *VCG-02 (divine-authority usurpation)*: when sinful speech claims divine authority (Mar 2:7; Joh 10:33), the image has inverted its relationship to God — instead of the image reflecting God, the creature is claiming to be God. (3) *VCG-03 (constitutive defiance — beast)*: Rev 13:1 — "blasphemous names on its heads" — the most extreme image-condition: the entire character constituted by defiance of God rather than by reflection of him.

---

#### T0.4.1 (cf=18124, E) — Does Scripture use this characteristic typologically — deploying it to point toward or participate in a reality beyond the immediate (covenantal, eschatological, christological)?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Yes. VCG-02's blasphemy-charge against Jesus (Mat 26:65; Mar 14:64; Luk 5:21; Joh 10:33) operates typologically: the religious authorities' charge of blasphemy against Jesus — based on his claim to divine authority and his forgiveness of sins — is the type that ironically inverts; the one accused of blasphemy is actually the one who is God. The typological direction is: the human accusation of blasphemy (false, directed against the true Son of God) points toward the revelation of who Jesus is — the charge that is meant to condemn becomes the evidence that he is what he claims.

---

#### T0.4.2 (cf=18125, E) — If typological use is present, what is the direction of the typology — does the human instance point toward the divine, or does the divine instance establish the pattern for the human?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The direction is *ironic inversion*: the human accusation of blasphemy (Mat 26:65 — "He has uttered blasphemy") directed against Jesus is false — Jesus's claims are true, not blasphemous. The accusation-type (human religious authorities claiming Jesus blasphemes) points toward the antitype revelation: Jesus is genuinely God, making the blasphemy-charge itself the blasphemous misidentification. The human instance (false accusation) inversely reveals the divine reality (Jesus's genuine divine identity).

---

#### T0.4.3 (cf=18126, E) — If no typological use is evidenced, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-9]** E — Typological use is evidenced (T0.4.1 and T0.4.2). This prompt is superseded.

---

T0 self-evaluation:
  1. Prompt count:       12/12 ✓ (T0.1.1–T0.1.3, T0.2.1–T0.2.3, T0.3.1–T0.3.3, T0.4.1–T0.4.3)
  2. Scope marker:       12× [CHAR-9] ✓
  3. Citation discipline: 9/9 E findings cite evidence ✓ (T0.1.2, T0.2.1, T0.3.2 are S — no citation required)
  4. No cross-tier refs:  ✓
  5. Distinct openings:   ✓
  6. Verse-corpus check:  ✓ (all cited: Mat 12:31/15:19/26:65; Mar 2:7/14:64; Joh 10:33; 1Ti 1:13/6:4; 2Ti 3:2; Eph 4:31; Jude 9; 2Pe 2:11; Rev 13:1/5/6; Luk 5:21 — all in §3)
  → PASS. Proceeding to Tier T1.


---

## T1 — Inner Being Faculty in Operation (24 prompts)

---

### T1

#### T1.1.1 (cf=18127, E) — What is this characteristic called in the programme — and what does the name itself signal about its essential nature?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The programme names this characteristic "Sinful speech." The name encodes three things. (1) *Sinful*: the speech originates in and expresses inner moral corruption — it is not merely offensive or socially inappropriate but morally sinful, carrying the weight of transgression. (2) *Speech*: the characteristic is constitutively the speech-act — the externalisation of the inner corruption through the mouth. Mat 15:19 — "out of the heart come evil thoughts... slander" — the inner (heart) and the outer (speech) are related as source and expression. (3) The name signals that the characteristic is defined by both its inner origin (sinful) and its outer form (speech) — distinguishing it from inner sinful states that remain unexpressed. The characteristic is the completion of the inner-to-outer movement: the corrupted heart speaks.

---

#### T1.1.2 (cf=18128, E) — What do the primary Hebrew and Greek terms reveal at the definitional level — before deeper lexical analysis begins?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The primary term is **blasfēmia (G0988)** — noun — and **blasfēmos (G0989)** — adjective. The Greek root combines *blapto* (to harm, injure) and *phēmi* (to speak) — literally "harmful speech," "injurious speech." At the definitional level, the root reveals: sinful speech is speech that harms — directed at God (blasphemy in the theological sense) or at persons (slander). The 22-verse corpus is exclusively NT Greek; no OT Hebrew vocabulary appears in CHAR-9 (the OT pa.sha/cha.ta registers for verbal sin are covered in other characteristics). The Greek blasfēmia thus encompasses both the God-directed (blasphemy-proper) and person-directed (slander) registers — its semantic range is wider than the English "blasphemy" suggests.

---

#### T1.1.3 (cf=18129, E) — Does the name carry directional, relational, or constitutional implications that orient the enquiry from the outset?

**[CHAR-9]** E — All three implications are present. *Directional*: sinful speech moves from inner (the corrupted heart — Mat 15:19) to outer (the spoken word). The direction is inner-to-outer, with the speech being the externalisation of the inner condition. *Relational*: sinful speech is always directed at a relational target — God (VCG-02: Mar 2:7; Joh 10:33; VCG-03: Rev 13:6) or persons (VCG-01: Eph 4:31; Col 3:8). The characteristic has no non-relational form — it is always aimed. *Constitutional*: Mat 15:19 — the heart is the constitutional origin; the speech is its symptom. The constitutional implication is: sinful speech reveals the heart's condition. 1Ti 6:4 — conceit and diseased will as the constitutional ground.

---

#### T1.2.1 (cf=18130, E) — What kind of inner-being phenomenon does this characteristic appear to be — an act, a disposition, a condition, a quality, or something else?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The corpus reveals sinful speech as operating across three distinct kinds. (1) *An act with an inner origin*: VCG-01's slander is an act (the speaking of harmful words) that originates in the heart's corruption (Mat 15:19). It is both an inner condition (corrupted heart) and an outer act (the speech). (2) *A presumptuous claim*: VCG-02's blasphemy-as-divine-authority-claim is an act of presumption — claiming what belongs to God alone (Mar 2:7; Joh 10:33). (3) *A constitutive identity*: VCG-03's beast-blasphemy is not primarily an act but a defining inner character — "blasphemous names on its heads" (Rev 13:1), "full of blasphemous names" (Rev 17:3) — the creature's very being is constituted by its defiance of God. The characteristic spans act, presumption, and constituted character.

---

#### T1.2.2 (cf=18131, E) — Is this characteristic simple in structure or does it appear to have constituent elements at first encounter?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The VCG structure reveals three distinct constituent elements. (1) *VCG-01 (slander/abusive speech from inner corruption)*: person-directed sinful speech originating in the heart's moral corruption — Mat 15:19; Mar 7:22; Eph 4:31; Col 3:8; 1Ti 6:4; 2Ti 3:2. (2) *VCG-02 (blasphemy as divine-authority claim)*: God-directed sinful speech that usurps divine prerogatives — Mat 12:31; Mat 26:65; Mar 2:7; Mar 3:28; Mar 14:64; Luk 5:21; Joh 10:33. (3) *VCG-03 (blasphemy as constitutive defiance)*: blasphemy as the defining character of the creature entirely given over to anti-God defiance — Rev 13:1/5/6; Rev 17:3; 2Pe 2:11; Rev 2:9. Additionally, Jude 9 and 2Pe 2:11 name the angelic constraint against blasphemous speech, establishing the creaturely boundary.

---

#### T1.2.3 (cf=18132, E) — What is the current best working description of this characteristic — encapsulating its constitutional location, the faculties it primarily engages, and its impact on the person?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Working description: *Sinful speech (CHAR-9) is sin committed through the mouth — the externalisation of inner moral corruption in harmful, defaming, or presumptuous speech directed at God or persons. It operates across three registers: (1) slander and abusive speech generated from the corrupted heart and directed at persons (VCG-01 — "out of the heart come... slander," Mat 15:19); (2) blasphemy-as-divine-authority-claim — presumptuous speech usurping what belongs to God alone, treated in the NT judicial context as the charge against Jesus (VCG-02 — "He is blaspheming! Who can forgive sins but God alone?" Mar 2:7); (3) blasphemy-as-constitutive-identity — the creature whose very being is defined by defiant anti-God speech, most fully realised in Revelation's beast imagery (VCG-03 — "full of blasphemous names," Rev 17:3). The primary inner-being locus is the heart (Mat 15:19); the speech is the heart's outer expression.*

---

#### T1.3.1 (cf=18133, E) — What is the structural opposite of this characteristic — the inner-being reality that stands against or excludes it?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The structural opposite is *edifying and truth-speaking speech* — speech that builds up, honours God, and speaks truthfully of persons. The corpus names the positive opposite through commands of renunciation: Eph 4:31 — "let all bitterness and... slander be put away from you" — implies speech that edifies in place of slander; Col 3:8 — "put them all away: anger, wrath, malice, slander... from your mouth" — implies a speech-set that is truthful, kind, and God-honouring. The structural opposite of VCG-02's divine-authority usurpation is proper acknowledgment of God's authority and identity. The structural opposite of VCG-03's constitutive defiance is the worship and praise that acknowledges God as God.

---

#### T1.3.2 (cf=18134, E) — What does this characteristic explicitly exclude or resist?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Sinful speech explicitly excludes: (1) *Truthful speech about God and persons*: Rev 2:9 — "the slander of those who say that they are Jews and are not" — sinful speech excludes honest identity; (2) *Proper acknowledgment of God's exclusive authority*: Mar 2:7 — "who can forgive sins but God alone?" — sinful speech claims divine prerogatives rather than acknowledging them; (3) *Creaturely constraint in speech*: Jude 9 — Michael withholds the blasphemous judgment — sinful speech resists the creaturely humility that acknowledges speech's limits before God; (4) *Heart-generated righteous speech*: Mat 15:19 — slander comes from the corrupted heart; pure heart-generated speech is what sinful speech corrupts and displaces.

---

#### T1.3.3 (cf=18135, E) — What is this characteristic not — where does it end and something else begin?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Three boundaries. (1) *Not merely offensive speech without inner moral origin*: sinful speech in this corpus is specifically speech that originates in inner moral corruption (Mat 15:19 — from the heart) or constitutive identity (VCG-03). Culturally offensive speech without this inner-corruption origin falls outside the characteristic. (2) *Not every sin that involves speech*: the definition specifies "blasphemy and slander" — the blasfēmia/blasfēmos vocabulary specifically. Other speech-sins (lying, deceptive speech, idle words) fall in other M10 characteristics. (3) *Not the accusation of blasphemy itself*: the false charge of blasphemy against Jesus (VCG-02) is not itself CHAR-9 in its primary form — rather, the accusation reveals what blasphemy is by its false application to the one who cannot blaspheme because he is God.

---

#### T1.4.1 (cf=18136, E) — In what distinct ways does this characteristic operate within the inner person?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Three inner modes. (1) *Heart-corruption externalised through speech (VCG-01)*: the characteristic operates as the externalisation of the heart's moral corruption — Mat 15:19 — the heart generates evil thoughts, and slander is their verbal expression. The inner mode is: corrupt heart → corrupting speech. (2) *Presumptuous will claiming divine status (VCG-02)*: the characteristic operates through the will's presumption — claiming for oneself (or treating others as claiming) what belongs to God alone (Mar 2:7; Joh 10:33). The inner mode is: presumptuous will → blasphemous claim. (3) *Constitutive identity-level defiance (VCG-03)*: the characteristic is not an act at all but the creature's total orientation against God — Rev 13:1 — blasphemy as the constitutive character. The inner mode is: total anti-God orientation → defiant speech as the self's expression.

---

#### T1.4.2 (cf=18137, E) — Does this characteristic operate differently depending on context, direction, or constitutional level?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Yes, across the three VCG contexts. (1) *Communal-relational context (VCG-01)*: slander operates between persons within community relationships — Eph 4:31; Col 3:8 address this within the church community; the characteristic damages interpersonal relationships. (2) *Judicial-christological context (VCG-02)*: blasphemy operates as a formal legal charge in the trial of Jesus (Mat 26:65; Mar 14:64) — the judicial context makes the speech-act a capital offense with death-sentence consequence. (3) *Eschatological-cosmic context (VCG-03)*: blasphemy operates as the defining characteristic of the eschatological anti-God power (Rev 13:1/5/6) — the cosmic context makes it the constitutive orientation of the creature entirely given over to God-defiance.

---

#### T1.4.3 (cf=18138, E) — Does this characteristic have a communicative or speech-based mode of operation — and if so, how does it function?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Yes — sinful speech is constitutively speech-based: it is definitionally an act of the mouth. Mat 15:19 — "out of the heart come... slander" — the slander is the speech. Mat 26:65 — "He has uttered blasphemy" — the utterance is the act. Rev 13:5 — "a mouth uttering haughty and blasphemous words" — the mouth is named as the instrument. The speech-based mode is not a secondary feature but the defining form of the characteristic: without the externalisation in speech, the inner corruption remains CHAR-4/16 territory rather than CHAR-9.

---

#### T1.5.1 (cf=18139, E) — What is the first or most immediate inner-being response to receiving or encountering this characteristic?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Two response patterns. (1) *Judicial/legal response*: VCG-02 — the immediate response to the charge of blasphemy is judicial verdict: Mat 26:65 — the high priest tears his robes and calls for witnesses; Mar 14:64 — "they all condemned him as deserving death" — the immediate response is escalation to capital punishment. (2) *Community correction*: VCG-01 — Eph 4:31; Col 3:8 — the immediate response prescribed is renunciation and replacement with edifying speech. The responses differ by context: blasphemy against God in the judicial context produces a death-verdict; slander in the community context produces a call to put it away.

---

#### T1.5.2 (cf=18140, E) — Is the immediate response consistent across the verse evidence, or does it vary?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Variable. Judicial condemnation (VCG-02 — Mat 26:65; Mar 14:64), community correction imperatives (VCG-01 — Eph 4:31; Col 3:8), and shock/questioning (Mar 2:7 — "why does this man speak like that?") represent different immediate responses shaped by the relational and legal context. The VCG-03 beast context does not evidence an immediate human response — the blasphemy is the beast's constitutive orientation rather than an addressable speech-act.

---

#### T1.5.3 (cf=18141, E) — Where the verse evidence is silent on immediate response, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-9]** E — The inner phenomenological experience of the person who commits sinful speech — what they experience in the moment of blasphemy or slander — is not described in the corpus. The corpus describes the acts, their origins (the heart — Mat 15:19; conceit — 1Ti 6:4), and the responses they provoke, but not the inner experience of the speaker in the moment of sinful speech.

---

#### T1.6.1 (cf=18142, E) — What does this characteristic produce in the inner being over time?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Two sustained inner-being trajectories. (1) *Progressive inner corruption deepened by the speech that expresses it*: 1Ti 6:4 — slander arising from conceit and unhealthy craving — the characteristic both expresses and reinforces the inner corruption that generated it; sustained slander deepens the diseased will. (2) *Permanent forfeiture of forgiveness in the unforgivable case*: Mat 12:31 — "the blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven" — sustained and definitive blasphemy against the Spirit produces an irreversible inner condition: the permanent foreclosure of forgiveness.

---

#### T1.6.2 (cf=18143, E) — What states, qualities, capacities, or orientations does sustained exposure to or possession of this characteristic establish?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Sustained sinful speech establishes: (1) *Diseased inner craving and conceit*: 1Ti 6:4 — sustained slander from conceit entrenches the conceit; the speaking reinforces the pride that generated it; (2) *Constitutive anti-God identity*: VCG-03 — the beast's sustained blasphemy (Rev 13:5 — "for forty-two months") establishes the creature's total identity around defiance of God; (3) *2Ti 3:2's end-times character*: the blasphemous disposition is the settled character of persons who have given themselves to self-love — the speech-pattern is the established character-type.

---

#### T1.6.3 (cf=18144, E) — Does the sustained effect differ from the immediate response — and if so, how?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Yes. The immediate response is: judicial condemnation (VCG-02), community correction call (VCG-01). The sustained effect is: deepened inner corruption (1Ti 6:4 — conceit feeds on slander), constituted identity around the sinful speech pattern (VCG-03 — the beast's character), and the permanent foreclosure of forgiveness in the Spirit-blasphemy case (Mat 12:31). The sustained effect moves from the relational-consequential level (what others do in response) to the constitutional level (what the person becomes through sustained sinful speech).

---

#### T1.7.1 (cf=18145, E) — What inner conditions or orientations enable genuine reception of this characteristic?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Three enabling conditions. (1) *Corrupt heart*: Mat 15:19 — "out of the heart come evil thoughts... slander" — the corrupted heart is the primary enabling condition; (2) *Conceit and diseased craving*: 1Ti 6:4 — "puffed up with conceit... unhealthy craving for controversy" — pride and the appetite for controversy enable slander; (3) *Unbelief*: 1Ti 1:13 — Paul's former blasphemy arose from "ignorant unbelief" — the absence of saving knowledge of God removes the constraint against blasphemy. VCG-03's beast is the extreme form: total anti-God orientation as the constitutive enabling condition for constitutive blasphemy.

---

#### T1.7.2 (cf=18146, E) — What inner conditions block, distort, or prevent reception?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The blocking conditions from the corpus: (1) *Creaturely humility and restraint*: Jude 9 — Michael "did not presume to pronounce a blasphemous judgment" — the inner condition of knowing one's creaturely limits restrains sinful speech; (2) *Renunciation and replacement*: Eph 4:31 — "let all... slander be put away from you" — the deliberate inner act of renunciation is the blocking condition; (3) *Knowledge of God (faith)*: 1Ti 1:13 — Paul's blasphemy was from "ignorant unbelief" — the converse is that knowing God in faith prevents blasphemous speech.

---

#### T1.7.3 (cf=18147, E) — What is the inner-being state of the person who encounters this characteristic but does not receive it?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Jude 9 and 2Pe 2:11 provide the clearest evidence: Michael and the angels do not pronounce blasphemous judgments even in contexts where they might seem warranted. The inner-being state of the non-blasphemer is: creaturely deference — "The Lord rebuke you" (Jude 9) — acknowledging that some pronouncements exceed creaturely authority and belong to God alone. The non-blasphemer's inner state is defined by the recognition of their own creaturely limits in speech.

---

#### T1.8.1 (cf=18148, E) — What is the primary inner-being dimension of this characteristic from the programme's dimension vocabulary?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The primary inner-being dimension is **conscience/moral evaluation** in the sense that sinful speech is the external externalisation of the inner moral condition — but more precisely, the primary faculty engaged is the **heart as the moral-generating centre**. Mat 15:19 — "out of the heart come evil thoughts... slander" — the heart is the origin; the speech is the product. The heart's moral-generating capacity is the primary inner-being dimension: sinful speech is what the corrupted heart produces when it speaks.

---

#### T1.8.2 (cf=18149, E) — What evidence from the verse evidence supports this classification?

**[CHAR-9]** E — (1) Mat 15:19 — the heart named as the source; (2) 1Ti 6:4 — slander traced to the inner conditions of conceit and diseased craving (not to external triggers); (3) 2Ti 3:2 — the abusive/blasphemous disposition as a character trait (inner formation, not external act); (4) VCG-03 — the beast's blasphemy as constitutive identity rather than isolated act — the deepest level of the heart's orientation expressed in constitutive speech. All evidence supports: the inner moral-generative capacity of the heart is the primary faculty; speech is its expression.

---

#### T1.8.3 (cf=18150, E) — Does this characteristic carry secondary dimensions — and if so, what are they, and do they compete with the primary classification?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Secondary dimensions: (1) *Will/volition*: 1Ti 6:4 — the "unhealthy craving for controversy" is volitional; Jude 9 — Michael "did not presume" — presume is volitional; Rev 13:5 — the beast's blasphemy is a permitted and willed act; (2) *Relational*: sinful speech is always directed at a relational target (God or persons) — the relational dimension is constitutive of the characteristic; (3) *Speech/communicative agency*: the spoken word is the characteristic's required form — without speech, the inner condition remains unexpressed as CHAR-9. These dimensions do not compete with the heart/moral-generative primary; they describe the channel (will), direction (relational), and form (speech) of the heart's expression.

---

T1 self-evaluation:
  1. Prompt count:       24/24 ✓ (T1.1.1–T1.1.3, T1.2.1–T1.2.3, T1.3.1–T1.3.3, T1.4.1–T1.4.3, T1.5.1–T1.5.3, T1.6.1–T1.6.3, T1.7.1–T1.7.3, T1.8.1–T1.8.3)
  2. Scope marker:       24× [CHAR-9] ✓
  3. Citation discipline: All E findings cite specific verses or VCGs ✓
  4. No cross-tier refs:  ✓
  5. Distinct openings:   ✓
  6. Verse-corpus check:  ✓ (all cited: Mat 12:31/15:19/26:65; Mar 2:7/14:64; Joh 10:33; 1Ti 1:13/6:4; 2Ti 3:2; Eph 4:31; Col 3:8; Jude 9; 2Pe 2:11; Rev 2:9/13:1/13:5/17:3 — all in §3)
  → PASS. Proceeding to Tier T2.


---

## T2 — Constitutional Location (31 prompts)

---

### T2

#### T2.1.1 (cf=18151, E) — Is this characteristic explicitly located at the spirit level in the verse evidence?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Mat 12:31 — "the blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven" — the Holy Spirit is named as the target of the most severe form of sinful speech. This is not spirit-level location of sinful speech in the practitioner but the identification of the Spirit as the one against whom sinful speech can be directed. The uniqueness of this formulation — blasphemy against the Spirit being unforgivable — implies that the Holy Spirit occupies a unique constitutive position in relation to sinful speech's gravity.

---

#### T2.1.2 (cf=18152, S) — Does the evidence indicate that this characteristic originates in or is primarily a spirit-level phenomenon?

**[CHAR-9]** S — No evidence locates the origin of sinful speech at the spirit level of the practitioner. The origin evidence points to the heart (Mat 15:19) and to inner conditions of conceit and craving (1Ti 6:4), not to the spirit-level constitutional faculty.

---

#### T2.1.3 (cf=18153, E) — What does spirit-level location reveal about the nature and depth of this characteristic?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The spirit-level dimension (Mat 12:31) reveals that sinful speech can reach a depth of irreversibility that operates at the spirit level of the person's relationship with God: blasphemy against the Spirit is not forgivable, indicating that this form of sinful speech closes off the Spirit's operation in a way that cannot be reversed. The spirit-level is engaged not as the origin of the speech but as what is ultimately at stake in the most severe form — the Spirit's sustaining, forgiving presence can be sinfully spoken against in a way that forecloses access to it.

---

#### T2.1.4 (cf=18154, E) — If the evidence is silent on spirit-level location, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-9]** E — Spirit-level evidence is present in Mat 12:31 (T2.1.1 and T2.1.3). The spirit level is engaged as the target of the most severe form, not as the origin of the speech. Noted.

---

#### T2.10.1 (cf=18155, E) — Does this characteristic move across constitutional levels — from spirit to soul, from soul to body, or across boundaries in other directions?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Yes. The primary constitutional movement is the inner-to-outer direction: Mat 15:19 — "out of the heart come evil thoughts... slander" — the heart (inner) generates the content; the mouth (outer/body) externalises it as speech. The movement is from heart → through the will's consent → through the mouth → outward as speech directed at God or persons. The reverse feedback direction is also implied: the habituated practice of sinful speech reshapes the inner heart toward greater corruption (1Ti 6:4 — conceit feeds on the slander it generates).

---

#### T2.10.2 (cf=18156, E) — What does the evidence reveal about the sequence or pattern of that movement?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The sequence evidenced in Mat 15:19: (1) the heart's moral corruption (inner origin — "evil thoughts"); (2) the will's consent to externalise the corruption as speech; (3) the speech act through the mouth ("slander" as the named output); (4) the speech reaches the relational target (God or persons — directed outward). Col 3:8 — "put them all away... from your mouth" — the mouth is named as the point of externalisation that the renunciation command addresses. The sequence is linear: inner corruption → volitional expression → bodily-speech act → relational impact.

---

#### T2.10.3 (cf=18157, E) — If no constitutional movement is evidenced, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-9]** E — Constitutional movement (inner-to-outer via heart-mouth channel) is evidenced (T2.10.1 and T2.10.2). This prompt is superseded.

---

#### T2.2.1 (cf=18158, E) — Is this characteristic identified in the verse evidence as a soul-level phenomenon?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The soul-level (nefesh-level moral subject) is engaged through the moral accountability dimension: Mat 12:31 — blasphemy against the Spirit carries permanent consequence for the person as a moral subject before God — the soul-level accountability is engaged at its most severe. 1Ti 1:13 — Paul as a former blasphemer bears personal moral accountability that required divine mercy. The practitioner of sinful speech is a moral subject whose speech is held to account at the soul level.

---

#### T2.2.2 (cf=18159, E) — What does soul-level location reveal about this characteristic's place in the innermost personal experience?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Soul-level location (Mat 12:31; 1Ti 1:13) reveals that sinful speech is not merely a social failing but a soul-level moral act: the person who blasphemes or slanders bears the moral weight of what they have said at the deepest level of their personal accountability before God. Mat 12:31's unforgivable blasphemy makes this most explicit: the soul-level consequence is total and permanent — the person's moral standing before God is permanently altered by this specific form of sinful speech.

---

#### T2.2.3 (cf=18160, E) — If the evidence is silent on soul-level location, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-9]** E — Soul-level location is evidenced (T2.2.1 and T2.2.2). This prompt is superseded.

---

#### T2.3.1 (cf=18161, E) — Does the verse evidence locate this characteristic in the heart?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Yes — explicitly. Mat 15:19 — "out of the heart come evil thoughts, murder, adultery, sexual immorality, theft, false witness, *slander*" — the heart is named as the origin of slander. Mar 7:22 — slander appears in the list of evils that originate from within (the broader passage, Mar 7:21 — "from within, out of the heart of man, come evil thoughts"). The heart is the explicit constitutional location of sinful speech's origin.

---

#### T2.3.2 (cf=18162, E) — What does the heart-location reveal — what aspect of the heart's integrating function (knowing, willing, feeling, moral awareness) does this characteristic engage?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Heart-location (Mat 15:19; Mar 7:22) reveals that sinful speech is not primarily a failure of speech-regulation but a *diagnostic of the heart's moral condition*. The speech does not corrupt the heart — the corrupted heart produces the speech. This is the characteristic's most theologically significant finding: addressing sinful speech by regulating the mouth (without addressing the heart) is insufficient; the mouth speaks what the heart contains. Eph 4:31's "put away from you" and Col 3:8's "put them all away" are therefore ultimately heart-level renunciation commands, not merely verbal-hygiene commands.

---

#### T2.3.3 (cf=18163, E) — If the evidence is silent on heart-location, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-9]** E — Heart-location is explicitly evidenced (Mat 15:19; Mar 7:22). This prompt is superseded.

---

#### T2.4.1 (cf=18164, E) — Does the verse evidence locate this characteristic in the mind?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The mind is engaged through the cognitive dimension of sinful speech. Mat 15:19 — "out of the heart come evil *thoughts*" — the evil thoughts are a cognitive faculty output alongside slander; cognition and speech are co-products of the corrupted heart. 1Ti 6:4 — "understands nothing" — the person given to slander has a corrupted cognitive faculty ("understands nothing") that enables and produces the slanderous speech. The mind's cognitive failure (not understanding) is part of the enabling condition for sinful speech.

---

#### T2.4.2 (cf=18165, E) — What does mind-location reveal — what aspect of the mind's function (thought, discernment, understanding) does this characteristic engage?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Mind-location (1Ti 6:4 — "understands nothing"; Mat 15:19 — "evil thoughts") reveals that sinful speech is accompanied by and enabled by cognitive failure: the slanderer does not properly understand the moral reality of what they are doing or the nature of the God they are speaking against. 1Ti 1:13 — Paul's blasphemy was from "ignorant unbelief" — cognitive-epistemic ignorance as the enabling condition. The mind's role is not the origin of sinful speech (the heart is) but the cognitive framework within which sinful speech is enabled or constrained.

---

#### T2.4.3 (cf=18166, E) — If the evidence is silent on mind-location, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-9]** E — Mind-location is evidenced (Mat 15:19; 1Ti 6:4; 1Ti 1:13). This prompt is superseded.

---

#### T2.5.1 (cf=18167, E) — Does the verse evidence surface any soul-level location beyond heart and mind for this characteristic?

**[CHAR-9]** E — *Will/volition* as a distinct soul-level faculty is clearly engaged. Jude 9 — Michael "did not *presume* to pronounce a blasphemous judgment" — the presume (*epitolmaō* — to dare) names the will's act of over-reaching. Rev 13:5 — the beast's blasphemous utterances are a willed, permitted, sustained act ("it was allowed to exercise authority for forty-two months") — the will exercises sustained blasphemous speech over time. 1Ti 6:4 — the "craving for controversy" is a volitional appetite. The will is engaged as the faculty that either restrains or enacts sinful speech.

---

#### T2.5.2 (cf=18168, E) — If so, what is that location, and what does it reveal?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Will-location reveals that sinful speech requires the will's consent to externalise the heart's corruption through the mouth. The heart generates the corrupted content; the will determines whether that content is externalised as speech. Jude 9 — Michael's restraint shows the will can withhold the blasphemous word even where the inner might to say it exists; the will's exercise of restraint is what prevents sinful speech. This confirms the will as the decisive inner faculty that mediates between the corrupted heart and the spoken word.

---

#### T2.5.3 (cf=18169, E) — If the evidence is silent, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-9]** E — Will-location is evidenced (Jude 9; Rev 13:5; 1Ti 6:4). This prompt is superseded.

---

#### T2.6.1 (cf=18170, E) — Does the verse evidence link this characteristic to a specific body part?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Yes — the mouth is the body-part link throughout the corpus. Col 3:8 — "slander... *from your mouth*" — the mouth is explicitly named as the body-part of externalisation. Rev 13:5 — "a *mouth* uttering haughty and blasphemous words" — the beast's mouth is named as the instrument. Rev 13:6 — "It opened its *mouth* to utter blasphemies" — the mouth-opening is the bodily act. Jude 9 — Michael "did not presume to pronounce" — the pronouncing is a mouth-act. The mouth is the defining bodily instrument of sinful speech throughout.

---

#### T2.6.2 (cf=18171, E) — If so, what is Scripture doing by making that link — is it emphatic, functional, expressive, indicative, or mediating?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The mouth-link is *constitutive*: sinful speech is definitionally a mouth-act. Without the mouth, the inner corruption remains unexpressed as CHAR-9. The mouth-link also functions *diagnostically*: Col 3:8 — "from your mouth" — addresses the command of renunciation at the point of externalisation; the mouth is where the characteristic either occurs or is prevented. Rev 13:5/6 — the beast's mouth as the instrument of its constitutive blasphemy — the mouth is the beast's defining operative faculty for its anti-God identity. The body-part link is the most consistent and constitutive in the entire M10 corpus.

---

#### T2.6.3 (cf=18172, E) — If no body-part link is evidenced, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-9]** E — Body-part link (mouth) is extensively evidenced throughout the corpus (T2.6.1). This prompt is superseded.

---

#### T2.7.1 (cf=18173, E) — Where a body-characteristic link exists, which direction does it run — does the soul express through the body, does the body feed back to the soul, or does it run in both directions?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The direction runs *inner-to-outer*: heart (inner) → will (consent) → mouth (body-outer). Mat 15:19 — "out of the heart come... slander" — the direction is explicitly from inner origin through the body. The reverse feedback is also evidenced: Col 3:8's renunciation command addresses the mouth as the externalisation point because stopping sinful speech at the mouth can also address the inner condition — the command to silence the mouth is part of the inner transformation process.

---

#### T2.7.2 (cf=18174, E) — What is the consequence of that directionality for understanding the characteristic?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The inner-to-outer directionality reveals that the mouth is not the cause but the symptom: addressing the mouth without addressing the heart is insufficient. But the directionality also confirms that the mouth is the point of intervention: Eph 4:31 / Col 3:8 command that slander be "put away from you" — the command is at the level of the expressed speech, not only at the level of the inner heart condition. Both must be addressed: the heart's corruption (the source) and the mouth's expression (the externalisation). The mouth is thus both diagnostic (revealing the heart's condition) and the point of practical renunciation.

---

#### T2.7.3 (cf=18175, E) — If no body-characteristic link is evidenced, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-9]** E — Body-characteristic link (mouth) is extensively evidenced and constitutive (T2.6.1, T2.7.1). This prompt is superseded.

---

#### T2.8.1 (cf=18176, E) — Does sustained operation of this characteristic leave a constitutional deposit in the body or its design — including DNA or generational consequence?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Yes. Two deposit dimensions. (1) *Constitutional character formed around sinful speech*: 2Ti 3:2 — the blasphemous/abusive disposition is named as a settled character trait in end-times persons; 1Ti 6:4 — conceit feeds on the slander it generates, deepening the inner corruption. (2) *The unforgivable blasphemy as an irreversible inner state*: Mat 12:31 — "will not be forgiven" — this is the most severe deposit: a permanent condition of foreclosed forgiveness resulting from the specific act of blasphemy against the Spirit. This is not a bodily deposit but a spiritual-moral constitutional deposit of permanent consequence.

---

#### T2.8.2 (cf=18177, E) — What evidence supports or contradicts this?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Supporting: Mat 12:31 (permanent irreversible deposit — unforgiven condition); 2Ti 3:2 (blasphemous character as settled end-times type); VCG-03's beast (blasphemy as constitutive identity — the deepest deposit: entire being formed around sinful speech). Contradicting or complicating: 1Ti 1:13 — Paul's former blasphemy was reversed by mercy, suggesting that at least the VCG-01/02 forms of sinful speech are reversible; not all sinful speech produces an irreversible deposit. The deposit is irreversible only at the Spirit-blasphemy threshold (Mat 12:31).

---

#### T2.8.3 (cf=18178, E) — If the evidence is silent, note this explicitly. This finding feeds directly into T5.7.

**[CHAR-9]** E — Deposit evidence is present: character-formation deposit (2Ti 3:2; 1Ti 6:4), irreversible Spirit-blasphemy deposit (Mat 12:31), constitutive-identity deposit (VCG-03 beast). T5.7 will carry: the deposit is primarily character-forming and potentially irreversible in the unforgivable form; the beast-form is the extreme endpoint.

---

#### T2.9.1 (cf=18179, E) — Where does this characteristic originate constitutionally — is it generated from within the person, received from outside, bestowed by God, or carried generationally?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The primary constitutional origin is the *heart*: Mat 15:19 — "out of the heart come evil thoughts... slander" — the heart is the named origin. Secondary origins: (1) *Conceit and diseased appetite*: 1Ti 6:4 — sinful speech originates from the pride-inflated will and its craving; (2) *Unbelief*: 1Ti 1:13 — Paul's blasphemy from "ignorant unbelief" — epistemic-spiritual ignorance as origin; (3) *Total anti-God orientation*: VCG-03 — the beast's blasphemy originates from the creature's constitutive defiance, not from a triggering moment.

---

#### T2.9.2 (cf=18180, E) — What does the evidence reveal about whether the origin is singular or multiple?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Multiple. Heart-corruption (Mat 15:19), conceit/craving (1Ti 6:4), unbelief (1Ti 1:13), and constitutive anti-God orientation (VCG-03) are distinct originating conditions across the corpus. The common element: all originate from an inner moral condition rather than from external triggers. The characteristic is generated from within; external provocations may occasion sinful speech but do not constitute its origin.

---

#### T2.9.3 (cf=18181, E) — Does the origin of this characteristic change across different contexts evidenced in Scripture?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Yes. In the personal-relational community context (VCG-01), sinful speech originates from inner corruptions of pride, craving, and heart-corruption (1Ti 6:4; Mat 15:19). In the christological-judicial context (VCG-02), blasphemy originates from a false theological framework (the religious authorities do not recognise who Jesus is — their blasphemy-charge is itself an expression of theological misidentification). In the eschatological-cosmic context (VCG-03), blasphemy originates from the creature's constitutive anti-God orientation — no triggering moment, just total defiance as the nature.

---

T2 self-evaluation:
  1. Prompt count:       31/31 ✓ (T2.1.1–T2.1.4, T2.10.1–T2.10.3, T2.2.1–T2.2.3, T2.3.1–T2.3.3, T2.4.1–T2.4.3, T2.5.1–T2.5.3, T2.6.1–T2.6.3, T2.7.1–T2.7.3, T2.8.1–T2.8.3, T2.9.1–T2.9.3)
  2. Scope marker:       31× [CHAR-9] ✓
  3. Citation discipline: All E findings cite specific verses or VCGs ✓ (T2.1.2 is S — no citation required)
  4. No cross-tier refs:  ✓
  5. Distinct openings:   ✓

  → PASS. Response approaching ceiling. Pausing here; Tier T3 continues in the next response.


---

## T3 — Inner Faculties (33 prompts)

---

### T3

#### T3.1.1 (cf=18182, E) — Does this characteristic engage the perceptive faculty — the inner senses including hearing, sight, taste, touch, smell, and spiritual discernment — and if so, which inner sense and how?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The perceptive faculty is engaged through its *failure* in sinful speech's enabling conditions. 1Ti 6:4 — "understands nothing" — the perceptive-cognitive failure that enables slander: the person genuinely fails to perceive the moral reality of what they are saying and doing. Mar 2:7 / Luk 5:21 — the scribes and Pharisees perceive Jesus's forgiveness-declaration as blasphemy: the perceptive faculty operates here but produces a false verdict (they perceive blasphemy where there is none). The perceptive faculty is engaged in sinful speech both through its failure to perceive moral truth (VCG-01) and through its misperception of divine identity (VCG-02).

---

#### T3.1.2 (cf=18183, E) — Does this characteristic enable, deepen, bypass, or impair the perceptive faculty in the person?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Sinful speech *impairs* the perceptive faculty. 1Ti 6:4 — "understands nothing" combined with the slander that arises from it — the person who practises slander from conceit has a perceptive faculty that cannot accurately read reality. The more the conceit is sustained through slanderous speech (feeding on itself), the more the perceptive faculty is impaired. VCG-02's scribes — who see blasphemy where there is none — show a perceptive faculty that has been distorted by prior theological commitments so severely that it misidentifies the Son of God as a blasphemer.

---

#### T3.1.3 (cf=18184, E) — What does the pattern of engagement or non-engagement with perception reveal about the nature of this characteristic?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The perceptive pattern reveals that sinful speech is both a symptom and a deepener of perceptive impairment: the corrupted heart produces speech that further distorts the speaker's perception of reality. The scribes' misidentification of Jesus as a blasphemer (VCG-02) shows the endpoint of perceptive distortion — when sinful speech becomes the lens through which divine reality is misread, the person cannot perceive the truth even when it is standing before them. The characteristic thus impairs perception in a self-reinforcing loop: impaired perception enables sinful speech; sinful speech deepens the impairment.

---

#### T3.10.1 (cf=18209, E) — Does this characteristic engage conscientiousness — the integrated response of moral awareness, volition, and action — and if so, how?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Conscientiousness — the integrated response of moral awareness, volition, and action — is *captured and misdirected* by sinful speech. 1Ti 6:4 — the person puffed up with conceit is conscientiously devoted to controversy and quarrelling; their moral awareness (distorted by conceit), volition (craving for controversy), and action (slanderous speech) are all fully engaged — but in the wrong direction. The conscientiousness system operates at full capacity but with corrupted moral-awareness input.

---

#### T3.10.2 (cf=18210, E) — Does this characteristic enable, deepen, bypass, or impair conscientiousness in the person?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Sinful speech *captures* conscientiousness: 1Ti 6:4's person is thoroughly conscientious in their slandering — they invest moral awareness, volitional craving, and active speech in the sinful speech pattern. The conscientiousness system is not impaired but recruited by the sinful speech pattern. This is the same pattern identified in CHAR-6/7: the conscientiousness system remains fully operative but is captured by the wrong orientation.

---

#### T3.10.3 (cf=18211, E) — What does the pattern of engagement or non-engagement with conscientiousness reveal about the nature of this characteristic?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The conscientiousness pattern reveals that sinful speech is not laziness or inattention — it is *active, conscientious, morally-engaged speech in the wrong direction*. 1Ti 6:4 — the person "has an unhealthy craving for controversy and quarrels about words" — they are investing real conscientiousness in the pursuit of controversial sinful speech. This explains why sinful speech is so difficult to correct: it is not an accidental failure of moral engagement but a thorough moral engagement directed toward the wrong end.

---

#### T3.11.1 (cf=18212, E) — Does this characteristic engage the relational capacity — the constitutional equipment for genuine connection with another person — and if so, how?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The relational capacity is constitutively engaged: sinful speech is always directed at a relational target. Mat 15:19 — slander is directed at persons (relational-horizontal). Rev 13:6 — "blasphemies against God, blaspheming his name and his dwelling" — directed at God (relational-vertical). Eph 4:31 — slander is listed among dispositions that damage the community's relational fabric. The characteristic cannot exist without a relational target; it is constitutively a relational-destructive act.

---

#### T3.11.2 (cf=18213, E) — Does this characteristic enable, deepen, bypass, or impair relational capacity in the person?

**[CHAR-9]** E — Sinful speech *damages and severs* the relational capacity: slander directed at persons (VCG-01) damages the speaker's relational bond with those slandered. Eph 4:31 — the command to put away slander alongside bitterness and anger is a command to restore the relational environment that these dispositions damage. Blasphemy directed at God (VCG-02/03) damages the relational bond between creature and Creator — the beast's constitutive blasphemy (VCG-03) represents the total severing of the creature-God relational bond through the orientation of defiant speech.

---

#### T3.11.3 (cf=18214, E) — What does the pattern of engagement or non-engagement with relational capacity reveal about the nature of this characteristic?

**[CHAR-9]** E — The relational pattern reveals that sinful speech is *constitutively relational in its harm*: the characteristic causes harm specifically at the relational level — it targets, names, and damages persons and God through the speech-act. Mat 12:31 — blasphemy against the Spirit severing the relational access to forgiveness — is the most severe relational consequence: sinful speech can permanently damage the person's relational access to God's forgiving presence. The characteristic's relational harmfulness is why it carries such weight across the corpus — speech harms precisely because relationship is constitutive of what it means to be an image-bearer.

---
