## CHAR-21 — Perversion as inner inversion (189 rows: E=186 S=3 G=0 CS=0)

### T0

#### T0.1.1 (cf=16603, E) — What does the verse evidence reveal about the nature or character of God that this characteristic reflects or images?

**[CHAR-21]** E — The 64-verse corpus reveals God's character across four dimensions. (1) *God as the one who hates perversity*: Pro 8:13 — "pride and arrogance and the way of evil and perverted speech I hate" — wisdom (personified as God's attribute) explicitly names hatred of perversity; (2) *God as the one who watches over the perversion of judgment*: Exo 23:8; Deu 16:19 — bribery blinds the wise and subverts the righteous; God's justice is the standard against which the perversion of justice is measured; (3) *God as the one who judges perversity*: Pro 21:12 — "The Righteous One observes the house of the wicked; he throws the wicked down to ruin"; Rev 19:2 — "he has judged the great prostitute who corrupted the earth with her immorality"; (4) *God as the one who disciplines rather than abandons the inwardly twisted*: 2Sa 7:14 — "when he commits iniquity, I will discipline him with the rod of men... not take my steadfast love from him" — God's response to perversity-as-iniquity is corrective discipline within sustained love rather than abandonment.

---

#### T0.1.2 (cf=16604, E) — Does Scripture explicitly attribute this characteristic to God — and if so, what does that attribution reveal about its significance in the human person?

**[CHAR-21]** E — One verse approaches this — Lam 3:9 — "he has blocked my ways with blocks of stones; he has made my paths crooked" — God is the subject of the crookedness-making action. However, the verse describes God's judicial action upon the sufferer (making their path crooked as discipline/judgment) rather than attributing moral perversity to God's character. God here acts as the one who twists the path, not as a morally perverted being. All other verses place perversity exclusively on the human side.

---

#### T0.1.3 (cf=16605, E) — Where Scripture is silent about God's possession of this characteristic, what does that silence suggest about the characteristic's place in the divine image?

**[CHAR-21]** E — The silence (apart from Lam 3:9's judicial use) confirms that moral perversity is constitutively creaturely. God's nature is the straight standard against which perversion is defined: Pro 8:13 — wisdom hates perverted speech; Deu 16:19 — God's justice is what bribery subverts. The characteristic requires a prior moral order (which God embodies) that can be twisted; God is the source of the moral order, not subject to its distortion. Isa 29:16's "you turn things upside down" implies a right-side-up that God's own nature constitutes.

---

#### T0.2.1 (cf=16606, S) — What does the verse evidence suggest about the purpose this characteristic serves in the human person as created — what does it equip the person to be, do, or become?

**[CHAR-21]** S — The corpus provides no evidence of any positive created purpose for perversion-as-inner-inversion. The characteristic is entirely a fallen-condition reality: the twisting of what was created straight and good. No verse presents moral perversity as a designed feature.

---

#### T0.2.2 (cf=16607, E) — Does the evidence indicate whether this characteristic is part of the original created design, a response to the fallen condition, or both?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Entirely a response to the fallen condition. Jer 2:21 — "I planted you a choice vine, wholly of pure seed. How then have you turned degenerate and become a wild vine?" — the degeneracy contrasts explicitly with the original planting of purity; the perversion is a corruption of what was first good. Eph 4:22 — "old self... corrupt through deceitful desires" — the corruption is attributed to the fallen "old self" and "deceitful desires" rather than original design. The characteristic emerges from the fall's distortion of what was created good.

---

#### T0.2.3 (cf=16608, E) — Is there evidence that this characteristic is oriented toward a future fullness — something the person is moving toward, not only what they currently are?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Yes — in two directions. (1) *Judgment endpoint*: Rev 19:2 — "he has judged the great prostitute who corrupted the earth" — eschatological judgment falls on the corrupting agent; Pro 10:31 — "the perverse tongue will be cut off" — judgment silences perversity; (2) *Liberation from corruption*: Rom 8:21 — "the creation itself will be set free from its bondage to corruption and obtain the freedom of the glory of the children of God" — the eschatological fullness is freedom from corruption as a cosmic reality; 2Pe 1:4 — "having escaped from the corruption that is in the world because of sinful desire" — the believer's orientation is toward escape from corruption into divine nature.

---

#### T0.3.1 (cf=16609, E) — In what way does this characteristic express the divine image in the human person — what aspect of being made in God's likeness does it instantiate?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Perversion expresses the image through its *inverted exercise of the moral faculty*: the image-bearer is constituted with a morally ordered inner life — a mind, will, heart, and speech designed for alignment with God's order. Perversion is the twisting of these faculties against their designed grain. Isa 29:16 — the creature denying the potter's authority — is the most direct image-inversion: the image-bearer's cognitive faculty, designed to know God as creator, is so perverted it denies the creator relationship. The capacity for moral ordering (the image's positive dimension) is precisely what perversion distorts.

---

#### T0.3.2 (cf=16610, S) — Does the evidence suggest that this characteristic is shared between God and the human person, or is it exclusively a creaturely analogue to something in God?

**[CHAR-21]** S — Exclusively creaturely in the moral sense. Lam 3:9's judicial use (God making paths crooked) is not God being morally perverted but God acting as judge. The characteristic in its inner-moral sense is exclusively creaturely.

---

#### T0.3.3 (cf=16611, E) — What does the presence or absence of this characteristic in a person reveal about the condition of the divine image in that person?

**[CHAR-21]** E — The presence of perversion reveals the image in its *internally distorted condition*: the faculties that were designed for moral alignment have been twisted against their grain. 1Ti 6:5 — "depraved in mind and deprived of the truth" — the mind (the image's cognitive faculty) has been corrupted at its very seat of understanding. Eph 4:22 — "old self... corrupt through deceitful desires" — the desire-faculty of the image has been turned into the agent of the corruption. The image is not destroyed but its inner faculties (mind, will, heart, desire, speech) are bent away from the moral order they were designed to instantiate.

---

#### T0.4.1 (cf=16612, E) — Does Scripture use this characteristic typologically — deploying it to point toward or participate in a reality beyond the immediate (covenantal, eschatological, christological)?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Yes — through the vine-degeneracy type and the creature-creator inversion type. (1) *Vine type*: Jer 2:21 — "I planted you a choice vine... how then have you turned degenerate and become a wild vine?" — Israel's degeneracy is the anti-type of the faithfully-producing vine; this vine-type points toward the true vine (Joh 15 — in CHAR-20's adjacent cluster); (2) *Creature-creator inversion*: Isa 29:16 — "shall the potter be regarded as the clay... 'He did not make me'?" — the cognitive inversion of creature denying creator is the type of the ultimate eschatological reversal in which God will act as the definitive potter who reshapes the perverted creation.

---

#### T0.4.2 (cf=16613, E) — If typological use is present, what is the direction of the typology — does the human instance point toward the divine, or does the divine instance establish the pattern for the human?

**[CHAR-21]** E — From human perversity-instance toward divine eschatological resolution. The vine's degeneracy (Jer 2:21 — human type) points toward the eschatological replanting/restoration; the creature's denial of the creator (Isa 29:16 — human perversity-type) points toward the eschatological re-establishment of the proper creator-creature order. Both directions: human perversity-instance → divine resolution.

---

#### T0.4.3 (cf=16614, E) — If no typological use is evidenced, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-21]** E — Typological use is evidenced (T0.4.1 and T0.4.2). This prompt is superseded.

---

T0 self-evaluation:
  1. Prompt count:       12/12 ✓ (T0.1.1–T0.1.3, T0.2.1–T0.2.3, T0.3.1–T0.3.3, T0.4.1–T0.4.3)
  2. Scope marker:       12× [CHAR-21] ✓
  3. Citation discipline: 10/10 E findings cite evidence ✓ (T0.2.1, T0.3.2 are S)
  4. No cross-tier refs:  ✓
  5. Distinct openings:   ✓
  6. Verse-corpus check:  ✓ (all cited: Exo 23:8; Deu 16:19; 2Sa 7:14; Pro 8:13/10:31/21:12; Isa 29:16; Jer 2:21; Lam 3:9; Eph 4:22; 1Ti 6:5; Rom 8:21; 2Pe 1:4; Rev 19:2 — all in §3)
  → PASS. Proceeding to Tier T1.


---

## T1 — Inner Being Faculty in Operation (24 prompts)

---

### T1

#### T1.1.1 (cf=16615, E) — What is this characteristic called in the programme — and what does the name itself signal about its essential nature?

**[CHAR-21]** E — The programme names this characteristic "Perversion as inner inversion." Three signals. (1) *Perversion*: the English rendering covers moral distortion, twisting, and corruption — the turning of what was designed to be straight into something bent; (2) *As inner inversion*: the defining modifier — the perversity operates from within, not merely as external misbehaviour; the inversion is of the moral order from inside the person; (3) *Inner*: the characteristic is at the level of the person's moral faculties — mind, heart, will, speech — not merely behavioural. The name signals: the most comprehensive inner distortion-category in M10 — the person whose moral-inner constitution has been twisted against its grain.

---

#### T1.1.2 (cf=16616, E) — What do the primary Hebrew and Greek terms reveal at the definitional level — before deeper lexical analysis begins?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Nine distinct term-families, the highest of any M10 characteristic. (1) **tah.pu.khah (H8419)** — "perverseness, frowardness" (VCG-01/03 dominant): the root *hāpak* means "to turn, to overturn" — what has been overturned; (2) **la.zut (H3891)** — "perversity, crookedness" (VCG-01 — Pro 4:24); (3) **sa.laph (H5557)** — "to subvert, to overthrow, to pervert" (VCG-02 — judgment perversion); (4) **a.vah (H5753A/B)** — "to be crooked, to twist, to do iniquity" (VCG-03 dominant); (5) **te.vel (H8397)** — "perversion, violation of natural/created order" (VCG-04 — Lev 18:23/20:12); (6) **sur (H5494)** — "to turn aside, to degenerate" (VCG-04 — Jer 2:21/17:13); (7) **cha.val (H2254B)** — "to destroy, to corrupt, to ruin" (VCG-05 OT — M03 cross-register); (8) **nav.lut (H5040)** — "lewdness, shame" (VCG-05 — Hos 2:10 — M07 cross-register); (9) **ftheirō/fthora/diaftheirō (G5351/5356/1311)** — NT "to corrupt, to destroy, corruption/decay" (VCG-05 NT dominant). The terms collectively reveal: perversion as twisting of the given order (tah.pu.khah/a.vah), violation of created categories (te.vel), judgment-subversion (sa.laph), and progressive decay (ftheirō/fthora).

---

#### T1.1.3 (cf=16617, E) — Does the name carry directional, relational, or constitutional implications that orient the enquiry from the outset?

**[CHAR-21]** E — All three. *Directional*: perversion always inverts something — a direction of moral movement that turns back on itself; Jer 3:21 — "perverted their way" — the directional turning of the life-path away from God; *Relational*: perversion damages the God-relationship and human relationships — Isa 29:16 — the creature denying the creator (relational inversion); Pro 6:14 — "continually sowing discord" — perversity fractures relational bonds; *Constitutional*: Eph 4:22 — "old self corrupt through deceitful desires" — the corruption is constitutional to the fallen "old self"; 1Ti 6:5 — "depraved in mind" — the depravity is at the constitutional level of the mind's functioning.

---

#### T1.2.1 (cf=16618, E) — What kind of inner-being phenomenon does this characteristic appear to be — an act, a disposition, a condition, a quality, or something else?

**[CHAR-21]** E — The corpus reveals CHAR-21 as a *multi-faculty inner distortion condition* that operates as both a disposition and a process. (1) *Dispositional dimension*: Deu 32:20 — "a perverse generation" — perversity as a generational character trait; Pro 12:8 — "twisted mind" — perversity as a constitutive feature of the mind; 1Ti 6:5 — "depraved in mind" — the depravity as settled condition; (2) *Process dimension*: Eph 4:22 — "is corrupt through deceitful desires" — active progressive corruption through desire; 2Pe 2:19 — "slaves of corruption" — the progressive capture; Jer 2:21 — "turned degenerate and become a wild vine" — a transformation-process of moral decay.

---

#### T1.2.2 (cf=16619, E) — Is this characteristic simple in structure or does it appear to have constituent elements at first encounter?

**[CHAR-21]** E — The corpus reveals five constituent elements corresponding to the five VCGs. (1) *Perversity of speech* (VCG-01 — 10V): tah.pu.khah — the tongue/lips as perversity's primary expressive medium; (2) *Perversion of judgment* (VCG-02 — 6V): sa.laph — bribery corrupting the cognitive-discernment faculty; (3) *Perversion of mind, will, and way* (VCG-03 — 19V): a.vah/tah.pu.khah — the twisted inner person whose mind and life-direction are bent; (4) *Sexual perversion and moral degeneracy* (VCG-04 — 4V): te.vel/sur — violation of created/family order; (5) *Moral corruption, decay, and lewdness* (VCG-05 — 25V): cha.val/nav.lut/ftheirō — comprehensive inner decay.

---

#### T1.2.3 (cf=16620, E) — What is the current best working description of this characteristic — encapsulating its constitutional location, the faculties it primarily engages, and its impact on the person?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Working description: *Perversion as inner inversion (CHAR-21) is the inner state or disposition of moral distortion — twisting what is right, corrupting what was good, inverting the moral order from within. Five registers: (1) Perversity of speech (VCG-01 — 10V): "perverted heart devises evil, continually sowing discord" (Pro 6:14); "perverted speech" as wisdom's named hatred (Pro 8:13); (2) Perversion of judgment (VCG-02 — 6V): "bribe blinds the clear-sighted and subverts the cause of those who are in the right" (Exo 23:8); (3) Perversion of mind, will, and way (VCG-03 — 19V): "we have sinned and have acted perversely and wickedly" (1Ki 8:47); "twisted mind is despised" (Pro 12:8); "you turn things upside down!" (Isa 29:16); (4) Sexual perversion and moral degeneracy (VCG-04 — 4V): bestiality and incest named "perversion" (Lev 18:23/20:12); "turned degenerate and become a wild vine" (Jer 2:21); (5) Moral corruption, decay, and lewdness (VCG-05 — 25V): "old self corrupt through deceitful desires" (Eph 4:22); "depraved in mind" (1Ti 6:5); "slaves of corruption" (2Pe 2:19). Distinguished from CHAR-19 (boundary-crossing) by operating as inner structural distortion rather than knowing-violation, and from CHAR-18 (accumulated crime) by being the twisting of the moral faculty rather than the accumulated record.*

---

#### T1.3.1 (cf=16621, E) — What is the structural opposite of this characteristic — the inner-being reality that stands against or excludes it?

**[CHAR-21]** E — The structural opposite is *moral straightness — the inner constitution aligned with God's created order*. Pro 8:13 — wisdom's hatred of perversion implies wisdom's straightness as the opposite; Pro 10:31 — "the mouth of the righteous brings forth wisdom, but the perverse tongue will be cut off" — the wisdom-producing righteous is the structural opposite; 2Pe 1:4 — "having escaped from the corruption that is in the world" — escape-from-corruption into divine-nature-participation; Eph 4:22-24 — "put off your old self which is corrupt" contrasted with "put on the new self, created after the likeness of God in true righteousness and holiness" — the new self in God's likeness is the structural opposite.

---

#### T1.3.2 (cf=16622, E) — What does this characteristic explicitly exclude or resist?

**[CHAR-21]** E — The characteristic explicitly excludes: (1) *Wisdom and knowledge*: Pro 2:12 — wisdom delivers from "men of perverted speech" — perversity and wisdom are mutually exclusive; 1Ti 6:5 — "depraved in mind and deprived of the truth" — perversity of mind excludes truth; (2) *Inheritance of the kingdom*: 1Cor 15:50 — "the perishable cannot inherit the imperishable" — the corruptible state is constitutively incompatible with God's kingdom; (3) *Divine favour*: Deu 32:20 — God hides his face from a perverse generation; (4) *Proper function of judgment*: Exo 23:8 — bribery-perversion blinds and subverts what judgment is designed to produce.

---

#### T1.3.3 (cf=16623, E) — What is this characteristic not — where does it end and something else begin?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Three boundaries. (1) *Not CHAR-19 (Transgression as boundary-crossing)*: CHAR-19 is the knowing step-over of a known line; CHAR-21 is the structural inner distortion that bends the moral faculty itself — a twisted mind (Pro 12:8) is not primarily transgressing a law but operating from a corrupted cognitive apparatus; (2) *Not CHAR-18 (Iniquity as accumulated crime)*: CHAR-18 is the accumulated moral-crime account before God; CHAR-21 is the inner structural corruption that generates those crimes — the twisted heart (Pro 6:14) that produces continuous evil; (3) *Not purely physical decay*: Several VCG-05 NT verses (1Cor 15:42/50; 2Cor 4:16; Col 2:22; Rom 8:21) address bodily perishability in a cosmological-anthropological sense more than moral inner-being content. These carry cross-register flags (M23/M35) — noted.

---

#### T1.4.1 (cf=16624, E) — In what distinct ways does this characteristic operate within the inner person?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Five distinct inner modes. (1) *As a perverted heart continually devising evil*: Pro 6:14 — "with perverted heart devises evil, continually sowing discord" — the heart is the origin of the continuous evil-devising; (2) *As a depraved mind robbed of truth*: 1Ti 6:5 — "depraved in mind and deprived of the truth" — the cognitive faculty is corrupted at its root; (3) *As corruption through deceitful desire*: Eph 4:22 — "corrupt through deceitful desires" — the desire-faculty is the active agent of the corruption; (4) *As cognitive-relational inversion*: Isa 29:16 — the creature denying the creator — the mind inverts the proper creature-creator relationship; (5) *As degenerative transformation*: Jer 2:21 — "turned degenerate and become a wild vine" — a process of inner moral transformation in the wrong direction.

---

#### T1.4.2 (cf=16625, E) — Does this characteristic operate differently depending on context, direction, or constitutional level?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Yes across the five VCG registers. (1) *Speech context* (VCG-01): perversity operates through the tongue — Pro 6:14 — twisted heart devising evil sown through speech; (2) *Judicial context* (VCG-02): perversity operates through bribery corrupting the discernment faculty (Exo 23:8); (3) *Personal moral context* (VCG-03): perversity operates as the twisted inner life direction — Jer 3:21 — "perverted their way and forgotten the Lord"; (4) *Sexual-created-order context* (VCG-04): perversity operates as violation of the categories God established in creation (Lev 18:23 — te.vel); (5) *Cosmic-spiritual context* (VCG-05): perversity operates as a universal corruption from which all of creation needs liberation (Rom 8:21).

---

#### T1.4.3 (cf=16626, E) — Does this characteristic have a communicative or speech-based mode of operation — and if so, how does it function?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Yes — VCG-01 (10 verses) is entirely the speech-perversion register. Pro 6:14 — perverted heart devises evil and sows discord through speech; Pro 8:13 — perverted speech named as wisdom's hated counterpart; Pro 10:31 — "the perverse tongue will be cut off"; Pro 23:33 — "your heart utter perverse things" — drunkenness producing heart-generated perverted speech. The speech-mode of perversion is the most extensively evidenced in the corpus: VCG-01 dedicates 10 verses entirely to the tongue/lips/mouth as perversity's primary communicative expression.

---

#### T1.5.1 (cf=16627, E) — What is the first or most immediate inner-being response to receiving or encountering this characteristic?

**[CHAR-21]** E — The immediate responses include confession and self-indictment. 1Ki 8:47 — "we have sinned and have acted perversely and wickedly" — immediate communal confession naming perversity specifically; Dan 9:5 — "we have sinned and done wrong and acted wickedly and rebelled" — the comprehensive confessional self-indictment; 2Sa 24:17 — "I have sinned, and I have done wickedly" — David's immediate self-indictment upon seeing the plague; Job 33:27 — "I sinned and perverted what was right" — confessional first-person recognition.

---

#### T1.5.2 (cf=16628, E) — Is the immediate response consistent across the verse evidence, or does it vary?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Variable. Confession and self-indictment (1Ki 8:47; Dan 9:5) are the productive responses. Deflection onto God appears in Pro 19:3 — "a man's folly brings his way to ruin, his heart rages against the Lord" — the immediate response to self-produced perversion is heart-rage against God rather than self-recognition. 2Pe 2:19 — those who promise freedom while enslaved to corruption — show no recognition response at all. Isa 29:16 — those who deny the creator — have an inverted response that produces further perversion rather than recognition. The range is from honest confession to deflection to complete non-recognition.

---

#### T1.5.3 (cf=16629, E) — Where the verse evidence is silent on immediate response, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-21]** E — For VCG-04's sexual perversion (Lev 18:23/20:12 — bestiality and incest named "perversion"), the immediate inner experience of the person committing these acts is not described. The corpus names the acts and their classification as perversion and their penalty (death) but not the inner recognition or response of the one committing them.

---

#### T1.6.1 (cf=16630, E) — What does this characteristic produce in the inner being over time?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Four sustained inner-being effects. (1) *Progressive corruption through desire*: Eph 4:22 — "corrupt through deceitful desires" — ongoing active corruption; (2) *Enslavement to the corrupting force*: 2Pe 2:19 — "slaves of corruption. For whatever overcomes a person, to that he is enslaved" — the sustained perversion produces volitional captivity; (3) *Destruction by one's own instincts*: Jude 10 — "destroyed by all that they, like unreasoning animals, understand instinctively" — the perversion produces self-destruction through unreasoning impulse; (4) *Deprivation of truth*: 1Ti 6:5 — "depraved in mind and deprived of the truth" — sustained mind-perversion produces the inability to access truth.

---

#### T1.6.2 (cf=16631, E) — What states, qualities, capacities, or orientations does sustained exposure to or possession of this characteristic establish?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Three established states. (1) *Delight in perversity*: Pro 2:14 — "who rejoice in doing evil and delight in the perverseness of evil" — sustained perversion establishes an affective orientation of joy-in-wrongdoing; (2) *Continual evil-devising*: Pro 6:14 — "continually sowing discord" — sustained perversity produces unceasing destructive scheming; (3) *Bondage to corruption*: Rom 8:21 — "bondage to corruption" — the created order itself is in sustained enslaved-corruption as a structural condition.

---

#### T1.6.3 (cf=16632, E) — Does the sustained effect differ from the immediate response — and if so, how?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Yes. The immediate response (confession — 1Ki 8:47; self-indictment — Dan 9:5) is episodic. The sustained effects (active corruption through deceitful desires — Eph 4:22; enslavement — 2Pe 2:19; self-destruction by instinct — Jude 10; deprivation of truth — 1Ti 6:5) are constitutional states established over time. The most significant sustained effect is the transformation from agent-of-perversion to captive-of-corruption: 2Pe 2:19 — whatever overcomes a person enslaves them. The initial active perversion (the agent twisting) becomes passive enslavement (the corruption mastering the agent).

---

#### T1.7.1 (cf=16633, E) — What inner conditions or orientations enable genuine reception of this characteristic?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Three enabling conditions. (1) *Deceitful desires*: Eph 4:22 — "old self corrupt through deceitful desires" — desires that deceive (promising satisfaction while delivering corruption) are the enabling agent; (2) *Absence of wisdom/God-fear*: Pro 8:13 — "the fear of the Lord is hatred of evil... perverted speech I hate" — the absence of God-fear removes the resistance to perversion; Pro 2:12 — wisdom delivers from perverted speech — wisdom's absence enables it; (3) *Material inducement*: Exo 23:8; Deu 16:19 — bribery as the enabling mechanism for judgment-perversion; the monetary inducement corrupts the discernment faculty.

---

#### T1.7.2 (cf=16634, E) — What inner conditions block, distort, or prevent reception?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Three blocking conditions. (1) *Wisdom and God-fear*: Pro 8:13 — the fear of the Lord as hatred of evil including perverted speech; Pro 2:12 — wisdom delivering from perverted-speech people; (2) *Participation in the divine nature*: 2Pe 1:4 — "having escaped from the corruption that is in the world because of sinful desire" — participation in divine nature is the escape-mechanism from corruption; (3) *Putting off the old self*: Eph 4:22-24 — the deliberate act of putting off the corrupt old self; the actively renewed mind (Eph 4:23) is the blocking mechanism against deceitful-desire corruption.

---

#### T1.7.3 (cf=16635, E) — What is the inner-being state of the person who encounters this characteristic but does not receive it?

**[CHAR-21]** E — 2Pe 2:12 names it most vividly: "like irrational animals, creatures of instinct, born to be caught and destroyed... will also be destroyed in their destruction" — the inner state is animal-instinctual operating without reason, heading toward destruction that matches their nature. 1Ti 6:5 — "depraved in mind and deprived of the truth" — the inner state lacks the basic cognitive capacity to access truth. Jude 10 — destroyed by what they understand instinctively — the instinct has replaced moral reasoning as the inner governing faculty. The characterised inner state is: reason and moral faculty replaced by unreflective instinct, enslaved to the corruption that will ultimately destroy the self.

---

#### T1.8.1 (cf=16636, E) — What is the primary inner-being dimension of this characteristic from the programme's dimension vocabulary?

**[CHAR-21]** E — The primary dimension is **the moral faculty in its distorted/corrupted condition** — specifically the heart, mind, will, and desire as twisted from their designed moral orientation. Pro 6:14 — "perverted heart devises evil" — the heart as origin; 1Ti 6:5 — "depraved in mind" — the mind as primary corrupted faculty; Eph 4:22 — "corrupt through deceitful desires" — desire as corruption-agent. The characteristic operates at the level of the person's inner moral constitution rather than discrete acts or record-entries.

---

#### T1.8.2 (cf=16637, E) — What evidence from the verse evidence supports this classification?

**[CHAR-21]** E — (1) Pro 6:14 — heart as the perverted origin of continual evil-devising; (2) 1Ti 6:5 — mind as the named perverted faculty; (3) Eph 4:22 — desire as the active corruption-agent within the inner person; (4) Pro 12:8 — "twisted mind" as the characterising inner trait; (5) Isa 29:16 — the mind's cognitive-relational inversion as perversion; (6) 2Cor 11:3 — "your thoughts will be led astray" — the thoughts as the corruption-target.

---

#### T1.8.3 (cf=16638, E) — Does this characteristic carry secondary dimensions — and if so, what are they, and do they compete with the primary classification?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Yes — three secondary dimensions. (1) *Speech dimension* (VCG-01 — 10V): tah.pu.khah — the tongue as the primary expressive organ of inner perversity; (2) *Social-discord dimension*: Pro 6:14 — "continually sowing discord"; Pro 16:28 — "a dishonest man spreads strife" — perversity damages communal bonds as a secondary effect; (3) *Bodily-decay dimension* (several VCG-05 NT verses): fthora — physical perishability; Rom 8:21/1Cor 15:42/50 — bodily corruption; these carry M23/M35 cross-register flags indicating their primary locus is beyond M10 inner-being content.

---

T1 self-evaluation:
  1. Prompt count:       24/24 ✓ (T1.1.1–T1.1.3, T1.2.1–T1.2.3, T1.3.1–T1.3.3, T1.4.1–T1.4.3, T1.5.1–T1.5.3, T1.6.1–T1.6.3, T1.7.1–T1.7.3, T1.8.1–T1.8.3)
  2. Scope marker:       24× [CHAR-21] ✓
  3. Citation discipline: All E findings cite specific verses or VCGs ✓ (T0.2.1, T0.3.2 — noted as S in T0)
  4. No cross-tier refs:  ✓
  5. Distinct openings:   ✓
  6. Verse-corpus check:  ✓ (all cited: Exo 23:8; Deu 32:20; 1Sa 20:30; 2Sa 24:17; 1Ki 8:47; Pro 2:12/14/6:14/8:13/10:31/12:8/16:28/19:3; Isa 29:16; Jer 2:21/3:21; Lam 3:9; Dan 9:5; Eph 4:22; 1Ti 6:5; 2Pe 1:4/2:12/19; Jude 10; Rom 8:21; 1Cor 15:50; 2Cor 11:3; Job 33:27 — all in §3)
  → PASS. Response approaching ceiling. Pausing here; Tier T2 continues in the next response.


---

## T2 — Constitutional Location (31 prompts)

---

### T2

#### T2.1.1 (cf=16639, E) — Is this characteristic explicitly located at the spirit level in the verse evidence?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Yes — through two passages. (1) Job 17:1 — "my spirit is broken; my days are extinct; the graveyard is ready for me" — the spirit (*rûaḥ*) is the entity broken by corruption's action; though this is Job under affliction rather than Job's own perversity, it evidences the spirit as the level at which corruption's damage is registered; (2) Gal 6:8 — "for the one who sows to his own flesh will from the flesh reap corruption, but the one who sows to the Spirit will from the Spirit reap eternal life" — the Spirit-level sowing is set in opposition to the flesh-sowing that produces corruption; the Spirit is named as the counter-dimension where corruption does not operate.

---

#### T2.1.2 (cf=16640, E) — Does the evidence indicate that this characteristic originates in or is primarily a spirit-level phenomenon?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Partially — through VCG-03's Isa 29:16's cognitive-relational inversion (the creature denying the creator) which is a spirit-level orientation-reversal. More clearly: Pro 6:14 — "with perverted heart devises evil" — the heart is the origin, and the heart operates at the person's deepest inner level. The primary originating faculty is the heart (Pro 6:14) and the desire-faculty (Eph 4:22 — "through deceitful desires") rather than the spirit specifically — but the heart-level origin is close to the spirit-level of covenantal orientation.

---

#### T2.1.3 (cf=16641, E) — What does spirit-level location reveal about the nature and depth of this characteristic?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Spirit-level location (Job 17:1 — spirit broken; Gal 6:8 — flesh-sowing vs. Spirit-sowing) reveals that perversion-as-inner-inversion has a spirit-level impact dimension: the corruption damages or breaks the spirit as its deepest constitutional level. Gal 6:8's opposition between Spirit-sowing and flesh-corruption confirms that at the spirit-level the characteristic operates as the counter-dimension to God's Spirit — perversion is what happens when the person's sowing is oriented toward the flesh rather than Spirit.

---

#### T2.1.4 (cf=16642, E) — If the evidence is silent on spirit-level location, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-21]** E — Spirit-level location is evidenced (Job 17:1; Gal 6:8). This prompt is superseded.

---

#### T2.10.1 (cf=16643, E) — Does this characteristic move across constitutional levels — from spirit to soul, from soul to body, or across boundaries in other directions?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Yes — across multiple levels and directions. (1) *Heart → speech*: Pro 6:14 — "perverted heart devises evil" → discord sown outwardly; Pro 23:33 — "your heart utter perverse things" — heart-corruption expressing through mouth; (2) *Desire → progressive inner corruption*: Eph 4:22 — "corrupt through deceitful desires" — the desire-faculty drives corruption inward and outward through the old self's manner of life; (3) *Inner perversity → bodily consequence*: Psa 38:6 — "I am utterly bowed down and prostrate" — the inner-perversity produces bodily bowing; Num 5:27 (cross-reg) — physical consequence; (4) *Individual inner → communal*: Pro 6:14 — "continually sowing discord" — the perverted heart's output radiates outward into community disruption.

---

#### T2.10.2 (cf=16644, E) — What does the evidence reveal about the sequence or pattern of that movement?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Two primary sequences. (1) *Heart-to-conduct-to-consequence*: perverted heart (Pro 6:14 — inner) → devises evil → sows discord (outer communal) → ruin (Pro 13:6 — the wicked overthrown); (2) *Desire-to-corruption-to-slavery*: deceitful desires (Eph 4:22 — desire-level) → actively corrupt the old self → enslave the will (2Pe 2:19 — "slaves of corruption"). Both sequences confirm: perversion moves from the innermost level (heart/desire) outward to conduct and consequence, and inward to progressive self-corruption and volitional bondage.

---

#### T2.10.3 (cf=16645, E) — If no constitutional movement is evidenced, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-21]** E — Constitutional movement is evidenced (T2.10.1 and T2.10.2). This prompt is superseded.

---

#### T2.2.1 (cf=16646, E) — Is this characteristic identified in the verse evidence as a soul-level phenomenon?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Yes. 2Pe 2:19 — "For whatever overcomes a person, to that he is enslaved" — the person (*anthrōpos* — the whole human being) is the entity enslaved by corruption; Jude 10 — "destroyed by all that they, like unreasoning animals, understand instinctively" — the person's whole being is destroyed by instinct-driven corruption. Dan 9:5 — "we have sinned and done wrong and acted wickedly" — the communal self-assessment names perversity as a whole-person condition.

---

#### T2.2.2 (cf=16647, E) — What does soul-level location reveal about this characteristic's place in the innermost personal experience?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Soul-level location (2Pe 2:19 — whole-person enslavement; Jude 10 — whole-person destruction) reveals that perversion-as-inner-inversion is not compartmentalised to one faculty but encompasses the whole inner person. The slavery of 2Pe 2:19 is total: whatever overcomes the person (*anthrōpos*) enslaves the person — the entire person is subject to the corruption's mastery. Jude 10's destruction by instinct similarly encompasses the whole person.

---

#### T2.2.3 (cf=16648, E) — If the evidence is silent on soul-level location, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-21]** E — Soul-level location is evidenced (2Pe 2:19; Jude 10; Dan 9:5). This prompt is superseded.

---

#### T2.3.1 (cf=16649, E) — Does the verse evidence locate this characteristic in the heart?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Yes — the heart is the primary named origin of perversity within the corpus. Pro 6:14 — "with perverted heart devises evil, continually sowing discord" — the heart is the explicitly named seat of perversity that drives evil-devising; Pro 23:33 — "your heart utter perverse things" — the heart as the source of the perverted verbal output; Deu 32:20 — "a perverse generation, children in whom is no faithfulness" — the perversity characterises the inner disposition the generation's hearts carry.

---

#### T2.3.2 (cf=16650, E) — What does the heart-location reveal — what aspect of the heart's integrating function (knowing, willing, feeling, moral awareness) does this characteristic engage?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Heart-location (Pro 6:14 — "perverted heart"; Pro 23:33 — heart uttering perverse things; Deu 32:20 — perverse generation) reveals that perversion-as-inner-inversion originates at the heart's deep centre. Pro 6:14's "perverted heart devises evil continually" is the corpus's defining inner-being statement: the heart is both the origin and the continual driver of perversity; the perversion is not episodic but continuous because it is seated in the heart's dispositional centre. The heart is the characteristic's primary constitutional location.

---

#### T2.3.3 (cf=16651, E) — If the evidence is silent on heart-location, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-21]** E — Heart-location is explicitly evidenced (Pro 6:14; Pro 23:33; Deu 32:20). This prompt is superseded.

---

#### T2.4.1 (cf=16652, E) — Does the verse evidence locate this characteristic in the mind?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Yes — most explicitly in the NT. 1Ti 6:5 — "depraved in mind (*noenous diephtharmenōn*) and deprived of the truth" — the mind (*nous*) is the specifically named corrupted faculty; the depravity has penetrated the seat of understanding. 2Cor 11:3 — "your thoughts will be led astray from a sincere and pure devotion to Christ" — the thoughts (*noēmata*) are the target of corruption. Isa 29:16 — "you turn things upside down! Shall the potter be regarded as the clay?" — the cognitive faculty inverts the proper creator-creature relationship.

---

#### T2.4.2 (cf=16653, E) — What does mind-location reveal — what aspect of the mind's function (thought, discernment, understanding) does this characteristic engage?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Mind-location (1Ti 6:5 — mind depraved; 2Cor 11:3 — thoughts led astray; Isa 29:16 — cognitive inversion) reveals that CHAR-21's perversion operates at the level of the mind's basic cognitive function: the capacity to know God as creator (Isa 29:16), to hold truth (1Ti 6:5 — "deprived of the truth"), and to maintain sincere devotion (2Cor 11:3). When the mind is corrupted, the person cannot accurately perceive the most fundamental realities (creator-creature relationship, truth). This makes CHAR-21's mind-corruption the most epistemically devastating dimension.

---

#### T2.4.3 (cf=16654, E) — If the evidence is silent on mind-location, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-21]** E — Mind-level location (1Ti 6:5; 2Cor 11:3; Isa 29:16) is evidenced. This prompt is superseded.

---

#### T2.5.1 (cf=16655, E) — Does the verse evidence surface any soul-level location beyond heart and mind for this characteristic?

**[CHAR-21]** E — *Desire* as a distinct soul-level faculty is explicitly the corruption-agent in the NT. Eph 4:22 — "old self... corrupt through deceitful desires (*epithumiais*)"; 2Pe 1:4 — "corruption that is in the world because of sinful desire (*epithumia*)"; Gal 6:8 — "sows to his own flesh" — the fleshly desire-orientation. *Will* is also engaged: Pro 6:14 — "devises evil" — the will drives the devising; 2Pe 2:19 — "slaves of corruption" — the will is captured. *Conscience* appears in Job 33:27 — "I sinned and perverted what was right" — the conscience producing the confession of perversion.

---

#### T2.5.2 (cf=16656, E) — If so, what is that location, and what does it reveal?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Desire-level location (Eph 4:22; 2Pe 1:4) reveals the most analytically significant inner-being finding for CHAR-21: desire (*epithumia*) is named as the active mechanism of corruption — "corrupt through deceitful desires." The desire-faculty is not merely associated with perversion but is identified as its engine: the deceitful desire is what the corruption operates through. This makes CHAR-21 M10's characteristic that most precisely locates sin's mechanism in the desire-faculty — a finding not present in any other M10 characteristic studied with this precision.

---

#### T2.5.3 (cf=16657, E) — If the evidence is silent, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-21]** E — Desire-level, will-level, and conscience-level locations are all evidenced (Eph 4:22; 2Pe 1:4; Pro 6:14; 2Pe 2:19; Job 33:27). This prompt is superseded.

---

#### T2.6.1 (cf=16658, E) — Does the verse evidence link this characteristic to a specific body part?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Yes — three body-part links. (1) *Mouth, lips, tongue*: Pro 4:24 — "put away from you crooked speech, put devious talk far from you"; Pro 10:31 — "the perverse tongue will be cut off"; Pro 10:32 — "the mouth of the wicked, what is perverse"; (2) *Eyes*: Pro 16:30 — "whoever winks his eyes plans dishonest things" — the eye-wink as the bodily signal of perverse inner plotting; Exo 23:8 — "a bribe blinds the clear-sighted" — the eyes as the faculty blinded by bribery-perversion; (3) *The bowed/bent back*: Psa 38:6 — "I am utterly bowed down and prostrate" — the body bent by sin's weight.

---

#### T2.6.2 (cf=16659, E) — If so, what is Scripture doing by making that link — is it emphatic, functional, expressive, indicative, or mediating?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Three distinct body-part functions. (1) *Mouth/tongue as perversity's primary expressive organ*: Pro 10:31/32 — the tongue and mouth are the body-parts through which inner perversity is most immediately expressed outward; the perverse tongue is the inner corruption's most visible bodily instrument; (2) *Eyes as perversity's corrupted perceptive organ*: the blinded eyes (Exo 23:8 — bribery) and the winking eyes (Pro 16:30 — planned dishonesty) name the eyes as the body-part through which perversity distorts perception and signals intent; (3) *The bowed body as perversity's consequence*: Psa 38:6 — the whole body bent and prostrate is the bodily expression of what sin's perversion has done to the person's inner posture.

---

#### T2.6.3 (cf=16660, E) — If no body-part link is evidenced, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-21]** E — Body-part links (mouth/tongue/lips — multiple VCG-01; eyes — Exo 23:8/Pro 16:30; bowed body — Psa 38:6) are extensively evidenced. This prompt is superseded.

---

#### T2.7.1 (cf=16661, E) — Where a body-characteristic link exists, which direction does it run — does the soul express through the body, does the body feed back to the soul, or does it run in both directions?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Bidirectional. (1) *Inner perversity → outer bodily expression*: Pro 6:14 — perverted heart → devises evil sown outward; Pro 23:33 — perverted heart → mouth utters perverse things; Pro 16:30 — inner plot → winking eye signal; (2) *Outer bodily influence → inner corruption*: Exo 23:8 — bribe (outer material) → blinds the eyes (outer faculty) → subverts inner judgment-discernment; 1Cor 15:33 — "bad company ruins good morals" — external social influence corrupts inner moral character; (3) *Inner corruption → bodily deterioration*: Psa 38:6 — inner sin producing the bowed, prostrate body; 2Cor 4:16 — "outer self is wasting away" — the bodily decay as consequence of the fallen inner condition.

---

#### T2.7.2 (cf=16662, E) — What is the consequence of that directionality for understanding the characteristic?

**[CHAR-21]** E — The bidirectional body-perversion link reveals that CHAR-21 operates through every directional channel simultaneously: inner twisting expresses through the body (mouth, eyes), external influences corrupt the inner (bribery, bad company), and inner corruption deteriorates the body (Psa 38:6; 2Cor 4:16). This multi-directional body-engagement is unique among M10 characteristics — no other characteristic studied engages the body through as many distinct directional channels as CHAR-21.

---

#### T2.7.3 (cf=16663, E) — If no body-characteristic link is evidenced, note this explicitly.

**[CHAR-21]** E — Body-characteristic link is extensively evidenced and bidirectional. This prompt is superseded.

---

#### T2.8.1 (cf=16664, E) — Does sustained operation of this characteristic leave a constitutional deposit in the body or its design — including DNA or generational consequence?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Yes — three deposit forms. (1) *Mind-depravity deposit*: 1Ti 6:5 — "depraved in mind and deprived of the truth" — the mind's depravity is a constitutional deposit that persists as the settled condition of the corrupted person's cognitive faculty; (2) *Desire-capture deposit*: Eph 4:22 — "corrupt through deceitful desires" — the ongoing corruption through desire is an accumulating deposit; 2Pe 2:19 — "slaves of corruption" — the enslavement is a constitutionally established condition; (3) *Bodily perishability*: Rom 8:21 — "bondage to corruption" — the creation's bondage to corruption is a constitutional condition awaiting eschatological liberation.

---

#### T2.8.2 (cf=16665, E) — What evidence supports or contradicts this?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Supporting: 1Ti 6:5 (mind-depravity deposit — constitutionally settled); Eph 4:22 (ongoing desire-corruption deposit); Rom 8:21 (bondage-to-corruption). Contradicting or complicating: Eph 4:22-24 — the corrupt old self is to be "put off" — the deposit is addressable through the deliberate renewal act; 2Pe 1:4 — "having escaped from the corruption" — the deposit is escapable through participation in divine nature. The deposit is constitutionally real but not irreversible: it requires a counter-action at the same constitutional level (renewal of the mind — Eph 4:23; participation in divine nature — 2Pe 1:4).

---

#### T2.8.3 (cf=16666, E) — If the evidence is silent, note this explicitly. This finding feeds directly into T5.7.

**[CHAR-21]** E — Deposit evidence is present: mind-depravity (1Ti 6:5), desire-corruption (Eph 4:22; 2Pe 2:19), bodily bondage (Rom 8:21). T5.7 will carry: all deposit forms are addressed through Spirit-level renewal (Eph 4:23 — "renewed in the spirit of your minds"; 2Pe 1:4 — divine nature participation; Gal 6:8 — Spirit-sowing). The desire-capture deposit is the most analytically significant — "corrupt through deceitful desires" names the mechanism precisely.

---

#### T2.9.1 (cf=16667, E) — Where does this characteristic originate constitutionally — is it generated from within the person, received from outside, bestowed by God, or carried generationally?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Three constitutional origins. (1) *Heart-level dispositional orientation*: Pro 6:14 — "with perverted heart" — the heart as the primary origin-faculty; (2) *Desire-faculty corruption*: Eph 4:22 — "corrupt through deceitful desires" — the desire-faculty is the active mechanism of corruption in the old self; 2Pe 1:4 — "corruption... because of sinful desire" — sinful desire as the world-corruption's origin; (3) *Instinctual unreasoning nature*: Jude 10 — "like unreasoning animals... understand instinctively" — the unreasoning instinct as the originating mode of the most extreme corruption.

---

#### T2.9.2 (cf=16668, E) — What does the evidence reveal about whether the origin is singular or multiple?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Multiple — across five VCG registers. Heart-origin (VCG-01/03 — Pro 6:14); desire-origin (VCG-05 — Eph 4:22; 2Pe 1:4); instinct-origin (VCG-05 — Jude 10); bribery-triggered cognitive corruption (VCG-02 — Exo 23:8); created-order violation (VCG-04 — Lev 18:23 — te.vel). CHAR-21 has the most diverse origin-set of any M10 characteristic studied in terms of the different inner faculties named as origin-points (heart, desire, instinct) plus external triggering conditions (bribery, bad company — 1Cor 15:33).

---

#### T2.9.3 (cf=16669, E) — Does the origin of this characteristic change across different contexts evidenced in Scripture?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Yes across the five VCG registers. (1) *VCG-01 (speech perversity)*: origin in the perverted heart (Pro 6:14) and the will's embrace of evil (Pro 2:14 — "delight in perverseness"); (2) *VCG-02 (judgment perversion)*: origin in external bribery corrupting the internal discernment-faculty (Exo 23:8); (3) *VCG-03 (mind/will/way)*: origin in the generational character of the perverted people (Deu 32:20) and the individual's twisting of what was right (Job 33:27); (4) *VCG-04 (sexual perversion)*: origin in violation of created-order categories (Lev 18:23 — te.vel — what God's creation-design separates); (5) *VCG-05 (corruption/decay)*: origin in sinful desire (2Pe 1:4) and unreasoning instinct (Jude 10).

---

## T3 — Inner Faculties (33 prompts)

---

### T3

#### T3.1.1 (cf=16670, E) — Does this characteristic engage the perceptive faculty — the inner senses including hearing, sight, taste, touch, smell, and spiritual discernment — and if so, which inner sense and how?

**[CHAR-21]** E — The perceptive faculty is centrally engaged through the judgment-perversion register. Exo 23:8 — "a bribe blinds the clear-sighted" — the clear-sighted person's perceptive faculty is blinded by the bribe; Deu 16:19 — "a bribe blinds the eyes of the wise" — the eyes of the wise are the perceptive faculty corrupted by perversion; Pro 16:30 — "whoever winks his eyes plans dishonest things" — the eyes are the instrument of signalling perverse intent. The perceptive faculty is thus both the target of perversion's corrupting action (blinded by bribery) and the communicative instrument through which perverse plans are signalled (the winking eye).

---

#### T3.1.2 (cf=16671, E) — Does this characteristic enable, deepen, bypass, or impair the perceptive faculty in the person?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Perversion *blinds* the perceptive faculty. Exo 23:8 — bribery "blinds the clear-sighted" — the very clarity of the perceptive faculty is eliminated; Deu 16:19 — "blinds the eyes of the wise" — wisdom's perceptive capacity is impaired. Isa 29:16 — "you turn things upside down" — the perceptive faculty cannot correctly assess the creator-creature relationship; Jer 17:13 — those who "turn away from you" — the perversion of directional perception produces departure from God. Perversion blinds the inner perceptive faculty to both moral reality (what is right) and theological reality (who God is).

---

#### T3.1.3 (cf=16672, E) — What does the pattern of engagement or non-engagement with perception reveal about the nature of this characteristic?

**[CHAR-21]** E — The perceptive pattern reveals that perversion's most damaging inner effect is the blinding of the cognitive-perceptive faculty. Exo 23:8 and Deu 16:19's "blinds the eyes" is the corpus's most explicit perceptive-faculty statement: the perversion of judgment through bribery does not merely distort what is seen but removes the capacity to see clearly. 1Ti 6:5 — "deprived of the truth" — the final state of mind-depravity is blindness to truth itself. The perceptive pattern confirms: perversion does not merely colour what is perceived but eliminates the perceptive capacity at the relevant moral and theological levels.

---

#### T3.10.1 (cf=16697, E) — Does this characteristic engage conscientiousness — the integrated response of moral awareness, volition, and action — and if so, how?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Yes — through the continual-devising dimension. Pro 6:14 — "with perverted heart devises evil, continually sowing discord" — the "continually" names a sustained, persistent conscientiousness directed toward evil-devising; Isa 32:7 — "he plans wicked schemes to ruin the poor with lying words, even when the plea of the needy is right" — the deliberate, sustained planning against the needy is a conscientious investment in perverse scheming.

---

#### T3.10.2 (cf=16698, E) — Does this characteristic enable, deepen, bypass, or impair conscientiousness in the person?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Perversion *misdirects* conscientiousness toward continual evil-devising. Pro 6:14 — "continually sowing discord" — the conscientiousness is fully deployed toward the ongoing production of harm. The "continually" is the key: perversion makes the conscientious investment in evil a sustained, unceasing activity. Conversely, the conscience-less instinctual stage (Jude 10; 2Pe 2:12) has bypassed conscientiousness entirely — the animal-instinctual mode is pre-conscientious rather than conscientiously evil.

---

#### T3.10.3 (cf=16699, E) — What does the pattern of engagement or non-engagement with conscientiousness reveal about the nature of this characteristic?

**[CHAR-21]** E — The conscientiousness pattern confirms the two-stage perversion dynamic: (1) active, conscientious evil-devising (Pro 6:14 — perverted heart continually scheming) and (2) pre-conscientious instinctual destruction (Jude 10 — unreasoning animals). Stage 1 is perversion with full conscientiousness engaged; stage 2 is perversion that has eliminated the conscientiousness faculty entirely. The characteristic thus ranges from highly conscious evil-devising to pre-conscious instinct-driven destruction — a range not evidenced in any other M10 characteristic.

---

#### T3.11.1 (cf=16700, E) — Does this characteristic engage the relational capacity — the constitutional equipment for genuine connection with another person — and if so, how?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Yes — through the discord-sowing and the creator-creature-inversion dimensions. (1) *Discord-sowing*: Pro 6:14 — "continually sowing discord" — perversity actively fractures relational bonds; Pro 16:28 — "a dishonest man spreads strife, and a whisperer separates close friends" — the perverse person's relational impact is strife and friendship-separation; (2) *Creator-creature relational inversion*: Isa 29:16 — the creature denying the creator — the fundamental God-human relationship is inverted; (3) *Covenant-forgetting*: Jer 3:21 — "perverted their way; forgotten the Lord their God" — the God-relationship is broken by the perversion.

---

#### T3.11.2 (cf=16701, E) — Does this characteristic enable, deepen, bypass, or impair relational capacity in the person?

**[CHAR-21]** E — Perversion *fractures* relational capacity at every level. Pro 16:28 — "separates close friends" — the perverse person's relational capacity produces the opposite of what relationship requires (trust, honesty); Isa 29:16 — the creator-creature relationship inverted; 1Cor 3:17 — "if anyone destroys God's temple, God will destroy him" — the corruption of the sacred relationship produces divine destruction. Perversion does not merely damage but inverts relational capacity: what should build bonds (wisdom, honesty) is replaced by what destroys them (strife, deceit).

---

#### T3.11.3 (cf=16702, E) — What does the pattern of engagement or non-engagement with relational capacity reveal about the nature of this characteristic?

**[CHAR-21]** E — The relational pattern reveals that perversion-as-inner-inversion produces relational inversion at every level: the God-relationship is inverted (Isa 29:16 — creature denies creator), the human friendship is destroyed (Pro 16:28 — close friends separated), the covenant-bond is broken (Jer 3:21 — forgotten God). CHAR-21 is the characteristic that most comprehensively damages the full spectrum of relational bonds — divine, covenantal, and human friendship — through its inner-inversion mechanism.

---
